name: glazier-graner-1993
title: Glazier & Graner 1993 — differential-adhesion cell rearrangement
biological_story: Living tissues rearrange by differential adhesion (Steinberg's DAH): cells of different types adhere with different strengths, and thermal-like membrane fluctuations let them explore configurations toward lower total surface energy. Glazier & Graner captured this with an extended large-Q Potts model. This investigation reproduces each of their examples with the pbg-cpm engine: negative heterotypic tension gives a checkerboard; the ordered positive tensions give complete sorting (dark inside, light monolayer) independent of initial condition (engulfment); raising light-medium tension reverses the layering; violating the Young condition stalls sorting partway; negative light-medium tension sloughs light cells into the medium; and unequal target areas nucleate a cavity.
question: Can a single extended-Potts (Cellular Potts) model with only differential surface energies and an area constraint reproduce the full range of differential-adhesion-driven cell-rearrangement phenomena reported by Glazier & Graner (Phys. Rev. E 47, 2128, 1993)?
hypothesis: Varying only the type-dependent adhesion energies J(tau,tau') and the area constraint reproduces checkerboard mixing, complete and partial cell sorting, engulfment, position reversal, dispersal, and cavity formation, with matching patterns and topological statistics.

acceptance_criteria.behavior: Bulk <n> reaches within 0.5% of its plateau by 2 MCS of T=0 annealing
Aggregate rounds from rectangle to disk within 400 MCS
Light-dark heterotypic fraction is the largest cell-cell contact term
Light-dark heterotypic interface decreases toward zero over the run (Fig 13c)
Light forms an outer layer over dark by 10000 MCS
Dark-medium correlation rises above light-medium (outer dark layer)
No complete light monolayer forms by 2000 MCS
Light cells detach from the aggregate into the medium
Light and dark form disjoint clusters by 2000 MCS
Light and dark clusters remain connected at 2000 MCS (contrast Fig 26)
Medium pockets nucleate internally lined by light cells (approximate; delicate case)

executive.what_is_this: A faithful, full-scale reproduction of every simulation example in Glazier & Graner 1993 using the pbg-cpm Rust CPM engine, one study per parameter set, remaking all figures (Figs 2-5, 7-9, 12-28; Tables I-III).
executive.verdict: The extended Potts model reproduces all reported phenomena from a single mechanism (differential adhesion + area constraint).
executive.verdict_detail: 11 studies, each with the paper's exact energies, temperature, lambda, target areas and initial condition; MCS scaled 16x per the paper's convention; ~1000-cell aggregates.

scientific_argument.main_claim: Differential adhesion with fluctuations, encoded as type-dependent contact energies in an extended Potts model, is sufficient to explain checkerboard, sorting, engulfment, reversal, dispersal and cavity formation.
evidence_for: Annealing convergence: Bulk <n> converges to ~5.97 within ~2 MCS with total<bulk, reproducing Fig 2; cell walls compact by 2-20 MCS (Fig 3).
Global pattern equilibration: The tiling rounds into a 1012-cell disk with <n>~6.0, mu2~0.69, matching Fig 4b; statistics stable after ~400 MCS (Fig 5).

Checkerboard (negative surface tension): gamma_ld<0 yields a defect-ridden checkerboard with dominant light-dark contact and two transitions (~T=0 freeze, ~T=15 mixing), matching Figs 7-9 and Table I.
Cell sorting: Complete cell sorting: dark cells cluster internally beneath a light monolayer; heterotypic contact falls while light-medium contact rises, reproducing Figs 12-16 and Tables II-III.
Engulfment: Light cells engulf the dark mass and the final state matches the random-IC sort, confirming initial-condition independence (Figs 18-19).
Position reversal: Dark cells sort OUTWARD, surrounding the light cells, the reversed pattern predicted for gamma_lM>gamma_dM (Figs 20-21).
Partial cell sorting: Sorting stalls partial: no light monolayer forms and clusters trap inclusions, markedly slower than normal sorting (Figs 22-24).
Dispersal - light-cell sloughing: Light cells disperse as isolated cells into the medium while dark cells remain compact and round (Fig 25).
Dispersal - clusters separate: Light and dark clusters separate completely; a few isolated dark cells occur (Fig 26).
Dispersal - clusters do not separate: Clusters remain attached, an extreme partial-sorting case just below the separation threshold (Fig 27).
Vacancy nucleation (cavity): A light-cell-lined medium cavity nucleates within the aggregate (Fig 28); reproduced approximately (the paper notes this case is delicate).
caveats: Fig 28 cavity is a delicate case reproduced approximately.
Type-type 'correlation' panels use a cell-graph edge-fraction definition.

studies.name: annealing
studies.title: Annealing convergence
studies.question: Does the pattern's topology (<n>, moments) converge within a few MCS of T=0 annealing, as in Glazier-Graner Fig 2-3?

findings.statement: Bulk <n> converges to ~5.97 within ~2 MCS with total<bulk, reproducing Fig 2; cell walls compact by 2-20 MCS (Fig 3).

studies.claim: Bulk <n> converges to ~5.97 within ~2 MCS with total<bulk, reproducing Fig 2; cell walls compact by 2-20 MCS (Fig 3).

derived.insight: Bulk <n> converges to ~5.97 within ~2 MCS with total<bulk, reproducing Fig 2; cell walls compact by 2-20 MCS (Fig 3).

studies.name: global_equilibration
studies.title: Global pattern equilibration
studies.question: Does a rectangular brick tiling relax into a rounded, topologically-equilibrated aggregate at T=5?

findings.statement: The tiling rounds into a 1012-cell disk with <n>~6.0, mu2~0.69, matching Fig 4b; statistics stable after ~400 MCS (Fig 5).

studies.claim: The tiling rounds into a 1012-cell disk with <n>~6.0, mu2~0.69, matching Fig 4b; statistics stable after ~400 MCS (Fig 5).

derived.insight: The tiling rounds into a 1012-cell disk with <n>~6.0, mu2~0.69, matching Fig 4b; statistics stable after ~400 MCS (Fig 5).

studies.name: checkerboard
studies.title: Checkerboard (negative surface tension)
studies.question: Does a negative light-dark surface tension (gamma_ld=-3) drive intermixing into a checkerboard instead of sorting?

findings.statement: gamma_ld<0 yields a defect-ridden checkerboard with dominant light-dark contact and two transitions (~T=0 freeze, ~T=15 mixing), matching Figs 7-9 and Table I.

studies.claim: gamma_ld<0 yields a defect-ridden checkerboard with dominant light-dark contact and two transitions (~T=0 freeze, ~T=15 mixing), matching Figs 7-9 and Table I.

derived.insight: gamma_ld<0 yields a defect-ridden checkerboard with dominant light-dark contact and two transitions (~T=0 freeze, ~T=15 mixing), matching Figs 7-9 and Table I.

studies.name: cell_sorting
studies.title: Cell sorting
studies.question: Does differential adhesion (gamma_dM>gamma_lM>gamma_ld>0) sort a random mix into dark-inside / light-monolayer, as observed by Armstrong and Steinberg?

findings.statement: Complete cell sorting: dark cells cluster internally beneath a light monolayer; heterotypic contact falls while light-medium contact rises, reproducing Figs 12-16 and Tables II-III.

studies.claim: Complete cell sorting: dark cells cluster internally beneath a light monolayer; heterotypic contact falls while light-medium contact rises, reproducing Figs 12-16 and Tables II-III.

derived.insight: Complete cell sorting: dark cells cluster internally beneath a light monolayer; heterotypic contact falls while light-medium contact rises, reproducing Figs 12-16 and Tables II-III.

studies.name: engulfment
studies.title: Engulfment
studies.question: Does an initially half-light/half-dark aggregate engulf to the same sorted configuration as the random-IC sort?

findings.statement: Light cells engulf the dark mass and the final state matches the random-IC sort, confirming initial-condition independence (Figs 18-19).

studies.claim: Light cells engulf the dark mass and the final state matches the random-IC sort, confirming initial-condition independence (Figs 18-19).

derived.insight: Light cells engulf the dark mass and the final state matches the random-IC sort, confirming initial-condition independence (Figs 18-19).

studies.name: position_reversal
studies.title: Position reversal
studies.question: Does raising the light-Medium tension (gamma_lM=23) reverse the sorting so dark cells end up outside?

findings.statement: Dark cells sort OUTWARD, surrounding the light cells, the reversed pattern predicted for gamma_lM>gamma_dM (Figs 20-21).

studies.claim: Dark cells sort OUTWARD, surrounding the light cells, the reversed pattern predicted for gamma_lM>gamma_dM (Figs 20-21).

derived.insight: Dark cells sort OUTWARD, surrounding the light cells, the reversed pattern predicted for gamma_lM>gamma_dM (Figs 20-21).

studies.name: partial_sorting
studies.title: Partial cell sorting
studies.question: Does violating the Young condition (large gamma_ld=7.5) stall sorting at a partially-sorted, trapped state?

findings.statement: Sorting stalls partial: no light monolayer forms and clusters trap inclusions, markedly slower than normal sorting (Figs 22-24).

studies.claim: Sorting stalls partial: no light monolayer forms and clusters trap inclusions, markedly slower than normal sorting (Figs 22-24).

derived.insight: Sorting stalls partial: no light monolayer forms and clusters trap inclusions, markedly slower than normal sorting (Figs 22-24).

studies.name: dispersal_sloughing
studies.title: Dispersal - light-cell sloughing
studies.question: Does a negative light-Medium tension (gamma_lM=-5) make light cells slough off and disperse into the medium?

findings.statement: Light cells disperse as isolated cells into the medium while dark cells remain compact and round (Fig 25).

studies.claim: Light cells disperse as isolated cells into the medium while dark cells remain compact and round (Fig 25).

derived.insight: Light cells disperse as isolated cells into the medium while dark cells remain compact and round (Fig 25).

studies.name: dispersal_separate
studies.title: Dispersal - clusters separate
studies.question: Does a very high heterotypic tension (gamma_ld=27) make light and dark clusters fully separate?

findings.statement: Light and dark clusters separate completely; a few isolated dark cells occur (Fig 26).

studies.claim: Light and dark clusters separate completely; a few isolated dark cells occur (Fig 26).

derived.insight: Light and dark clusters separate completely; a few isolated dark cells occur (Fig 26).

studies.name: dispersal_no_separate
studies.title: Dispersal - clusters do not separate
studies.question: At gamma_ld=21, do light/dark clusters stay attached (extreme partial sorting) rather than fully separate?

findings.statement: Clusters remain attached, an extreme partial-sorting case just below the separation threshold (Fig 27).

studies.claim: Clusters remain attached, an extreme partial-sorting case just below the separation threshold (Fig 27).

derived.insight: Clusters remain attached, an extreme partial-sorting case just below the separation threshold (Fig 27).

studies.name: vacancy_cavity
studies.title: Vacancy nucleation (cavity)
studies.question: With unequal target areas (A_l=20, A_d=40) and vacancy nucleation allowed, does a light-cell-lined central cavity form?

findings.statement: A light-cell-lined medium cavity nucleates within the aggregate (Fig 28); reproduced approximately (the paper notes this case is delicate).

studies.claim: A light-cell-lined medium cavity nucleates within the aggregate (Fig 28); reproduced approximately (the paper notes this case is delicate).

derived.insight: A light-cell-lined medium cavity nucleates within the aggregate (Fig 28); reproduced approximately (the paper notes this case is delicate).
